message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic, caused by the severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-Cov-2), has registered more than 234 million confirmed cases and more than 4.7 million deaths throughout the world until October 2, 2021. During the last few months, a significant number of reports of COVID-19 in patients with rheumatic diseases have been published. In this study the objective is to report the clinical characteristics of Chilean patients with rheumatic diseases and COVID-19 reported in the “Global Rheumatology Alliance” (GRA) physician registration platform.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Chilean patients with rheumatic diseases and COVID-19 were included in the Covid-19 GRA physician-reported registry.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>54 patients were included. The most common primary rheumatic disease was rheumatoid arthritis (RA) with 28 cases (51.9%). 30 patients (55.6%) used corticosteroids, of which 20 (66.7%) used a dose of 10 mg or less. 33 patients (61.1%) only used conventional DMARDs, 4 (7.4%) only biological, and 6 (11.1%) the combination. A total of 35 patients (64.8%) had to be hospitalized. 2 patients (3.7%) died. 26 patients of the 35 hospitalized (74.2%) required some type of ventilatory support, of which 5 (19.2%) required non-invasive and 8 (30.8%) invasive mechanical ventilation or extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (ECMO).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Most of included Chilean rheumatic patients were hospitalized, with a low mortality rate but with a high percentage of patients requiring at least non-invasive mechanical ventilation.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Key Points</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="simple">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>-</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>The most common primary rheumatic disease was rheumatoid arthritis (RA) followed by lupus (LES)</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>-</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>Most of the included Chilean rheumatic patients were hospitalized, with a high percentage of patients requiring at least non-invasive mechanical ventilation, but with a low mortality rate.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:label>-</jats:label>
                      <jats:p>Worsening of arthralgias or activation of the rheumatic disease was not reported.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Clinical characteristics of Chilean patients with rheumatic diseases and COVID-19: data from the Covid-19 Global Rheumatology Alliance physician-reported registry

reference.unstructured: Johns Hopkins University. COVID-19 Dashboard by the Center for Systems Science and Engineering (CSSE) at Johns Hopkins University [Available from: https://coronavirus.jhu.edu/map.html. Accessed October 2, 2021.
World Health Organization. Global Health Observatory Data [Available from: https://www.who.int/gho/publications/world_health_statistics/en/. Accessed October 2, 2021.

reference.article-title: Rheumatic disease and COVID-19: initial data from the COVID-19 Global Rheumatology Alliance provider registry

reference.unstructured: Ugarte-Gil M. et al. Characteristics associated with Covid-19 in patients with Rheumatic Disease in Latin America: data from the Covid-19 Global Rheumatology Alliance physician-reported registry. Global rheumatology by PANLAR. Sept 2020.

Strangfeld A , Schäfer M , Gianfrancesco MA , et al. Factors associated with COVID-19 related death in people with rheumatic diseases: results from the COVID-19 Global Rheumatology Alliance physician-reported registry
